secondary_archetype: Domain / Platform PM (Private Credit / Capital Markets)

level_signals: 8-10 years in private credit, capital markets, asset management, or financial services technology
Expected to shape domain direction and operate at a strategic level
Compensation range $110,000-$188,750 consistent with senior/lead IC at a large financial institution
Expected to partner with and present to the firm's most senior professionals

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8-10 years in private credit, capital markets, asset management, or financial services technology
Demonstrated domain understanding of institutional lending and credit investment workflows
Strong cross-functional leadership across business, technology, and servicing teams
Familiarity with operating within regulated financial institutions
Highly polished executive presence and sophistication to gain credibility with the firm's most senior professionals and provide leadership, guidance, and direction to a team.
Great communication skills and ability to work with people from a variety of backgrounds
Define and execute the multi-year Private Credit product vision aligned to Alpha strategy
Ability to travel as required for client engagements (-20%), including internationally

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: MBA, CFA, CAIA, or equivalent preferred
Private markets experience is a plus
Experience with user research initiatives as part of product discovery
Experience working within a component library system, and a demonstrated critical eye toward system maintenance and improvement
A portfolio that showcases your track record and dedication to continuous improvement
Desire to work in a high-growth environment

scope.surface: Charles River Private Markets platform — Private Credit domain (origination, portfolio management, servicing, reporting)
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS / platform for institutional asset managers and investors, within State Street Alpha ecosystem
primary_metrics_inferred: Client adoption within private credit segment
Revenue growth in private credit segment
Platform capabilities coverage across private credit lifecycle
Integration depth with Alpha ecosystem and State Street services
core_responsibilities: Define and execute the multi-year Private Credit product vision aligned to Alpha strategy
Establish scalable operating models across the private credit lifecycle
Drive client adoption and revenue growth within the private credit segment
Partner with Sales, Coverage, and Implementation to support commercialization
Own the end-to-end private credit domain strategy across origination, portfolio management, servicing, and reporting
Ensure platform capabilities support institutional investment decision-making, risk oversight, and performance transparency
Align private credit workflows with enterprise investment book of record and servicing environments
Partner with CRIMS and Alpha leadership to enable total portfolio transparency
Ensure private credit capabilities integrate with custody, fund administration, and reporting services
Support data governance, control, and regulatory alignment consistent with State Street standards
Embed operational resilience and regulatory awareness into product design
Maintain strong governance and documentation standards

green_flags: Clear domain ownership — end-to-end private credit lifecycle
Role is strategic and high-visibility within a large, established institution
Comprehensive benefits package described including 401K match, medical, dental, vision
Explicit mention of flexible work program
red_flags: Compensation range is wide ($78,750 spread), indicating significant uncertainty in level or significant location-based variation
No mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for international candidates
Travel requirement up to 20% including internationally may be burdensome
Component library and user research requirements seem mismatched for a senior/lead private credit PM role — may indicate scope confusion or copy-paste from a different JD
Years range listed as '8-10 years' which is atypical — implies a ceiling as well as a floor, potentially excluding very senior candidates
Role posted under Burlington, MA but JD does not clarify remote/hybrid options explicitly

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Lead', years range 8-10, comp range, and strategic scope)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; inferred false as no direct reports mentioned, though leadership of team is implied culturally)
location.type (inferred as onsite from Burlington, MA city listing; no hybrid/remote language in body)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description of Charles River Private Markets within State Street Alpha ecosystem)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated explicitly in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from JD statement 'If the candidate would ultimately work outside of the primary location above, the applicable range could differ')
ambiguities: The JD includes experience with 'component library system' and 'user research initiatives as part of product discovery' which seem unusual for a senior Private Credit PM role and may be copy-paste artifacts from a different JD template.

Years range '8-10 years' is stated as both a minimum and apparent maximum — treated as minimum_value=8 with max noted; the upper bound may simply define the target seniority band rather than exclude more experienced candidates.
No explicit remote/hybrid statement; location.type set to 'onsite' based on Burlington, MA listing, but flexible work program is mentioned generically in benefits — ambiguity not resolved.

Managing people: JD says 'provide leadership, guidance, and direction to a team' in the executive presence bullet but does not explicitly list people management as a responsibility — left as manages_people: false with ambiguity noted.
